# scopes set-alias-suffix

Command: `scopes set-alias-suffix`

The `scopes set-alias-suffix` command lets you create or update an alias suffix.

## Example

This example creates an alias suffix with the name `org1` in the scope `o_1234567890`:

```shell-session
$ boundary scopes set-alias-suffix -id o_1234567890 -alias-suffix org1
```

## Usage

<CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

```shell-session
$ boundary scopes set-alias-suffix [options] [args]
```

</CodeBlockConfig>

### Command options

- `-id=<string>` - The ID of the scope for which you want to create the suffix.
You can also specify the scope using the **BOUNDARY_SCOPE_ID** environment variable.
- `-alias-suffix` - Specifies the string that you want to use as the suffix to append to aliases in the designated scope.
The `alias-suffix` can be a hostname or a DNS-like string.

@include 'cmd-option-note.mdx'
